title: Labour Institutions (Minimum Wage for Private Sector) Order, 2025
title_en: Labour Institutions (Minimum Wage for Private Sector) Order, 2025

provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: This Order may be cited as the Labour Institutions (Minimum Wage for Private Sector) Order, 2025 and shall come into force on the 1 st day of January, 2026.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: This Order shall apply to all employees and employers in the private sector .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this Order, unless the context requires otherwise— “ domestic work ” means work performed in or for a household under employment relationship or employment agreement; “ agriculture ” includes crop production, forestry activities, fishing, animal husbandry, primary processing, storage, operation or transportation in agricultural undertaking, by or on behalf of the operator of that undertaking, including cooperative societies which are related to agricultural production; “ collective bargaining agreement ” means a written agreement concluded by a registered trade union and an employer or registered employers’ association on any labour matter; “ employee ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Employment and Labour Relations Act ; [Cap. 366] “ domestic worker ” means a person engaged in domestic or household work under an employment relationship or employment agreement; “ employer ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Employment and Labour Relations Act ; [Cap. 366] “ private sector ” means any sector other than public sector; “ energy ” includes all processes relating to the production or supply of energy from any source; “ mining operations ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Mining Act , but shall not include processes related to the production of salt or limestone. [Cap. 123]

provisions.title: Sectors and areas
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, minimum wage shall be paid in respect of sectors and areas prescribed in First Schedule. (2) The calculation of comparable salary rates shall be determined as prescribed in the First Schedule to the Employment and Labour Relations Act . [Cap. 366] (3) The minimum wage rates specified in the Second Schedule shall be regarded as the minimum wage payable to employee in the respective sector or area, and an employer may pay such employee an amount above the minimum wage prescribed in respective sector or area. (4) More favourable terms on the minimum wages rates shall be improved through employment contracts or collective bargaining agreements.

provisions.title: Employment standards
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of this Order, employment standards applicable to all employees in each specified sector or area shall be as specified under Part III of the Employment and Labour Relations Act , or as stipulated in employment contracts or collective bargaining agreements. [Cap. 366] (2) An employee shall in addition to annual paid leave, be entitled to a leave travel allowance once in every two years of continuous service with the same employer . (3) An employee shall be entitled to a subsistence allowance when required by his employer to work outside his work station according to the agreement entered upon between employer and employee . (4) Without prejudice to the generality of this paragraph, an employee who is a truck driver shall be entitled to an allowance to cover mileage, longer stay outside the work station, loading and unloading of cargo according to negotiations entered between drivers and employers.

provisions.title: Persons enjoying better terms
provisions.content: Where any employee to whom the provisions of this Order applies is, at the commencement of this Order, entitled to receive wages at a higher rate than those prescribed or provided for under this Order, that employee shall for so long as is employed by the same employer , continue to receive such higher wages and favourable terms.

provisions.title: Revocation
provisions.content: The Labour Institutions Minimum Wage Order, 2022 is hereby revoked. [GN. No. 687 of 2022] First Schedule (Made under paragraph 4(1)) Sectors and areas to benefit from minimum wage 1. Agricultural sector. 2. Health sector. 3. Communications sector. 4. Domestic work or services. 5. Hotel and hospitality services. 6. Private security services. 7. Energy sector. 8. Transport and shipping sector. 9. Construction sector. 10. Mining sector. 11. Private school services. 12. Trade and finance sector. 13. Industrial sector. 14. Sports, arts, entertainment, gaming activities. 15. Waste collection, processing and disposal activities. 16. Other sectors or areas not specified in this Order. Second Schedule (Made under paragraph 4(3)) Minimum wage rates No. Sectors and Areas Time 1. Agricultural Sector— (a) Crop or animal production and undertaking or activities related to agriculture; Hourly 897 Daily 6,731 Weekly 40, 386 Fortnightly 80,722 Monthly 175,000 (b) Forestry and deforestation of trees; Hourly 949 Daily 7,115 Weekly 42,692 Fortnightly 85,385 Monthly 185,000 (c) Fishing and fish farming or aquaculture. Hourly 410 Daily 3,077 Weekly 18,462 Fortnightly 36,923 Monthly 80,000 5.
